cards.prompt: Sustained allocation triggers a gen0 collection with no GC.Collect() call: the GC runs on its own
cards.code: using System; int before = GC.CollectionCount(0); var sink = new object[16]; for (int i=0;i<2_000_000;i++){ sink[i%16] = new byte[64]; } int after = GC.CollectionCount(0); Console.WriteLine($"ranAutomatically={after>before}");

cards.prompt: GC guarantee: new managed objects start with clean (zeroed) content
cards.code: using System; var a = new int[4]; Console.WriteLine($"zeroed={a[0]==0 && a[1]==0 && a[2]==0 && a[3]==0}");

cards.prompt: Real bytes allocated from the managed heap for a byte[1000]: exactly 1024 (payload + object header, 8-byte aligned)
cards.code: using System; long a = GC.GetAllocatedBytesForCurrentThread(); var o = new byte[1000]; GC.KeepAlive(o); long b = GC.GetAllocatedBytesForCurrentThread(); Console.WriteLine(b - a);
